$schema: Every session MUST update this file as its LAST action. Never edit fields of units you did not work on.

legend.rule: A phase's gate may be set to 'open' only when: all its units are 'verified' AND all reports in its gate_reports list exited 0.

last_session.unit: U-R.9 (NEW: onboard-NIC engine handling, Finding C) + Finding A availability-gate port into BaseCommand/Add/Replace + Finding B add_command_test DB-scenario fix + full DB-backed sweep against the scratch DB
last_session.note: DB WAS REACHABLE this session (XAMPP mysqld restarted, scratch credential per U-B.3 handoff; passed via GOLDEN_DB_* env only, production .env untouched, all flags stayed off). U-R.9 (implemented, NOT self-certified verified): ResourceCatalog::provides()/consumes() no longer throw on synthetic onboard-{mb}-{n}/onboard-nic-{mb24}-{n} spec_uuids (skip -- mirrors legacy lane-budget skip PcieLaneBudgetValidator:186, and no structured power field); sfp_port provision now RESOLVED via the parent board's networking.onboard_nics through new ResourceCatalog::providesOnboardNic() called from TargetState::resources() (parent_id first, uuid-prefix board match for json-fallback rows; fail-open [] like NICPortTracker::resolveOnboardNicSpecs). New DB-free test tests/unit/onboard_nic_engine_test.php 23/23. Fleet sweep EXECUTED before/after: 93 replays, unexplained 53 -> 5 (all 48 onboard rule_exceptions gone; the 5 survivors are cpu.socket_match bad-data configs 9dda0039/bbdd2549 -- CPU 545e143b 'LGA 4189' on LGA4677 boards, plus DDR4 RAM on a DDR5 board in bbdd2549; triage options presented to owner, NO expected_diffs entry, NO data fix without approval). Finding A CLOSED at code level: BaseCommand::assertInventoryAvailability() ports legacy checkComponentAvailability + line-745 override protocol (override_used bypasses everything incl. failed, is_virtual bypass, in-use-in-THIS-config passes), called from both Add/ReplaceComponentCommand buildTarget post-lock; DB-backed tests prove failed unit -> component_unavailable, in-use-elsewhere -> component_unavailable naming the holder, override_used bypasses. Finding B CLOSED: add_command_test.php dryRun()-pre-checks fixture pairs, asserts validation_blocked as a legitimate outcome (revision unchanged, no row), blocked path deliberately runs before the green add. FULL SWEEP: 16 unit/rule suites ALL PASS, 10 regression + 2 api suites exit 0 (remaining SKIPPEDs are PRIOR-session fixture-gated scenarios: rows-path motherboard fixture absent pre-backfill, Finding-2 chain gap, 2-connection race scenarios -- listed in handoff), characterization vs checked-in baseline: 12/12 overlapping configs byte-identical (3 configs present in the old baseline no longer exist in the scratch DB -- noted, baseline RESTORED). ReplaceComponentCommand end()-by-reference notice fixed. Everything this session: implemented, never verified; gates unchanged. (Prior session's note preserved in migration/handoffs/SESSION-20260712-P6-P7-FOLLOWUP.md.) OWNER AUTHORIZATION ON RECORD (extended to P7 this session): P2/P3/P4/P5/P6/P7 gates all stay closed on their own human/time-based preconditions regardless of code completeness. ENGINE_MODE/STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED/COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED stayed off/unset in production throughout. FIXED the independent verify record's Finding 1: handleAddComponent's shadow branch previously never ran the legacy add (undefined $result, would have broken every add under COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED=shadow) -- now mirrors handleRemoveComponent's pattern, comparing the command verdict against the legacy call's REAL outcome. Wrote real (not placeholder) DB-backed scenario code into all four tests/regression/*_command_test.php files, gated by a live scratch-DB connection check -- self-skip with an honest SKIPPED message this session (local MySQL still unreachable: 'Access denied for root@localhost, using password: NO' -- confirmed NOT a stopped-service issue this time, root truly has no known working credential in this environment; owner chose to have this session write seeders + code rather than chase DB access further). Added scripts/verify/fleet_parity_sweep.php (offline fleet-wide engine-vs-legacy replay reusing characterize_compatibility.php's own extraction loop + ServerBuilder's existing ENGINE_MODE=shadow hook/ShadowRunner -- not executed this session, confirmed to fail closed with a clear message when the DB is unreachable rather than a false green). Self-reviewed U-C.4 (ReplaceComponentCommand) against its pack -- no defects found, TargetStateBuilder::withAdd's append-only contract confirmed correct for the end()-based new-row-id lookup; still 'implemented' per this session's own instruction (never mark verified). Did NOT attempt Finding 2 (draft/building->finalized edge-table gap) -- documented where it bites in finalize_command_test.php's new DB scenario and in the P6 handoff instead, per explicit instruction. P7 (08-api-adapters): U-A.1 REDESIGNED as flag-gated per owner decision (deviation documented in migration/08-api-adapters/DEPRECATION.md) -- the advisory pre-check block in handleAddComponent is now skipped only at CommandLayer::mode()==='enforce' (mirrors U-C.5's precedent), NOT deleted unconditionally as the pack's literal text specified; the handler-level SFP auto-assign SQL was NOT deleted (AddComponentCommand has no equivalent yet -- a real feature-parity gap, flagged, not silently cut). U-A.2 shipped: server-replace-component/server-transition-status new additive actions (both gated behind CommandLayer::mode() !== 'off', a deliberate addition to the pack since neither command has ever run against production data), expected_revision->409-with-current-revision on all four mutation handlers, quantity<1 rejected with 400, quantity>1 mapped to N sequential command dispatches in one outer transaction (enforce mode only). U-A.3 shipped: VerdictShim.php (Verdict->legacy envelope, const rule_id->error_type map, RAM enrichment from memory.downclock, unknown rule ids fall back to compatibility_failure never a 500) wired into all four mutation handlers' CommandFailed catch blocks. New seeder database/seeders/2026_07_12_001_add-server-replace-transition-permissions.sql (server.replace/server.transition ACL rows, mirrors server.edit/server.create grants) -- shown to the owner, NOT run. Full DB-free regression sweep re-run at the end of this session (all green, see handoff for the full list) -- config_component_repository_test.php (a PRE-EXISTING test, not touched this session) remains the one file that still fatally requires a live DB connection rather than self-skipping; not a regression introduced here. All units this session: implemented, NOT independently verified (per this repo's own convention) -- a DB-capable session is needed to actually execute every scenario written this session and this migration's several prior sessions.

fourth_session_2026_07_12.unit: Sec4 owner decision (CPU spec fix, Option 2) + fleet sweep GREEN-on-socket/RED-on-memory.type + 3 fixture batches (remove/replace/finalize_command_test.php) + two-connection concurrency scenarios
fourth_session_2026_07_12.note: Owner picked SEC4 OPTION 2: ims-data/cpu/Cpu-details-level-3.json CPU 545e143b socket 'LGA 4189' -> 'LGA 4677' (factually correct for Sapphire Rapids Platinum 8480+), edited in both the canonical ims-data/ and its C:\tmp\ims-data scratch mirror. Fleet sweep re-run confirmed exactly the predicted outcome: all 5 cpu.socket_match diffs vanished (52 identical, up from 51), bbdd2549's previously-shadowed memory.type diff surfaced (4 unexplained, all on bbdd2549) -- DOCUMENTED with the config's full contents (DDR4 UDIMM non-ECC RAM 897472c6 on a DDR5-ECC-only X13DRG-H board) and NOT auto-resolved, per instruction; sweep stays RED by design pending an owner pick between an expected_diffs entry and a config-repair seeder. Built 3 in-transaction DB fixture batches that were previously fixture-gated SKIPs: (1) remove_command_test.php's rows-path motherboard blocked/cascade scenario (real motherboard+cpu inventory rows inserted as config_components rows inside the test's own rolled-back transaction); (2) replace_command_test.php's board A(LGA3647)->B(LGA2011-3) stranding scenario (blocks on cpu.socket_match, old board confirmed still live post-dryRun) and NIC-with-SFP re-anchor scenario (dependency.blocked_removal confirmed NOT firing once a motherboard+chassis presence fixture was added to isolate the signal from an unrelated structural-orphan requirement); (3) finalize_command_test.php's two-connection concurrency scenarios using two genuinely separate PDO connections against throwaway configs this session created and explicitly tore down (real commits, not rollbacks, since a second connection can't observe an uncommitted mutation or be blocked by a lock the first hasn't taken) -- 409 real-revision-after-concurrent-mutation (conn1 really commits a RemoveComponentCommand bumping revision 0->1, conn2 with the stale pre-mutation revision gets CommandFailed('revision_mismatch',...,409)) and finalize-race-blocks-under-lock (conn1 holds server_configurations' FOR UPDATE lock uncommitted, conn2 with innodb_lock_wait_timeout=1 gets CommandFailed('command_exception',...) via BaseCommand's catch-all, then succeeds past the lock once conn1 releases it). FULL SWEEP RE-RUN: all 28 unit+rules+regression+api suites exit 0 (every previously-SKIPPED DB-backed scenario in the three touched files now runs for real); characterization regenerated vs checked-in baseline -- 10/12 overlapping configs byte-identical, the other 2 (9dda0039, bbdd2549) differ ONLY in the now-resolved 'CPU socket LGA 4189 incompatible...' issue text disappearing (expected fallout of this session's own spec fix, not drift), same 3 gone uuids as every prior session, baseline RESTORED byte-for-byte and diffed clean against the real repo's untouched copy; fleet_parity_sweep reproducible across two independent runs (93 replays, 0 threw, identical=52 expected=37 unexplained=4, all memory.type on bbdd2549). tests/api/*'s remaining DB-backed criteria (golden response-shape, 409-over-real-HTTP, serial-targeted remove, two v2 actions) are unconditionally SKIPPED in the test file itself -- genuinely need a live HTTP context with CommandLayer::mode() !== 'off', which production rules forbid enabling, so left untouched and flagged rather than worked around. Everything this session: implemented, never self-certified verified. Production .env untouched; ENGINE_MODE/STATE_MACHINE_ENABLED/COMMAND_LAYER_ENABLED stayed off/unset throughout. Still open: bbdd2549 memory.type owner decision (P5 gate blocker), Finding 2 (draft/building->finalized edge gap, unchanged), chassis A->B bay revalidation fixture (not attempted), tests/api/*'s live-HTTP-gated criteria, U-C.6/U-B.4/P2/P3 soak preconditions (unchanged, human/time-gated).

fifth_session_2026_07_12.unit: Owner decision executed (CONFIG-REPAIR SEEDER for bbdd2549) + baseline re-blessed + chassis fixture written + U-X.1 plan; INCOMPLETE due to mid-session tooling outage
fifth_session_2026_07_12.note: Owner picked CONFIG-REPAIR SEEDER for the bbdd2549 memory.type residue. database/seeders/2026_07_12_002_repair-bbdd2549-ddr4-to-ddr5-ram.sql written (DDR4 897472c6 x2 -> DDR5 ECC RDIMM a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef x2 in ram_configuration JSON + defensive raminventory rows-path handling), shown, NOT run. EXECUTED proof via rolled-back-transaction replay (before this session's tooling outage): repair applied uncommitted on one PDO connection, bbdd2549 replayed through validateComponentAddition on the SAME connection -- both RAM lines flip from engine-blocked(memory.type) to identical; the config's 2 other pre-existing diffs (motherboard-singleton, pcie-slots-occupied) untouched/already-expected-bucketed; rolled back, scratch DB confirmed byte-identical after. Predicts fleet_parity_sweep unexplained 4->0 once the seeder actually runs in production. tests/golden/compatibility_baseline.json EXECUTED re-capture: ran characterize_compatibility.php against the (un-repaired) scratch DB, structurally diffed vs the old checked-in file -- confirmed the ONLY diff across all 12 overlapping configs is the stale 'LGA 4189' socket text disappearing on 9dda0039/bbdd2549 (exact predicted fallout of the fourth session's CPU spec fix; bbdd2549's DDR4/DDR5 memory.type text correctly still present since the RAM repair was not applied), same 3 pre-existing gone-config uuids as every prior session. COMMITTED as the new checked-in golden (real tree file now IS the fresh 12-config/93-replay capture, was 15/100). Chassis A->B bay-revalidation fixture WRITTEN into tests/regression/replace_command_test.php (chassis A=Dell R740 16x2.5in bays 327e585c, chassis B=Hitachi DS220 0x2.5in/12x3.5in bays 4981e5a2 flipped Status=1 in-transaction, fresh 2.5in SSD storageinventory row a3b4c5d6 inserted as occupant, third distinct config to avoid fixture collision, asserts storage.bay_capacity blocks) but **NOT EXECUTED -- UNVERIFIED, do not trust as green**. U-X.1 plan written (migration/09-cutover/U-X.1-PLAN-20260712.md, planning only, P8/U-X.1 stays not_started): confirms P7 gate closed (P8's own stated prerequisite) so U-X.1 cannot begin; flags real pack-vs-reality drift -- getConfigurationDetails/getConfigComponents line numbers stale (2248/5106 actual vs pack's 2149/4850), and getConfigComponents() is NOT a caller of extractComponentsFromJson() at all (a second independent hand-rolled extraction, different output shape, drops onboard NICs and sfp_configuration, single call site is the virtual-config-import MUTATION flow not a read entrypoint) -- pack's premise that it's a router call site alongside getConfigurationDetails is wrong, flagged with a recommendation to exclude it from U-X.1 scope. MID-SESSION TOOLING OUTAGE (honest account): after the above, Bash/PowerShell command execution (spawning php.exe/mysql.exe specifically; shell builtins kept working) became unavailable for the rest of the session despite dozens of retries over ~30 minutes, never recovering. As a direct result: the chassis fixture above was NOT run (design-reviewed by hand only); the full sweep re-run (all unit/rule/regression/api suites + fleet_parity_sweep.php) was NOT performed this session -- the last CONFIRMED full-green sweep remains the FOURTH session's (28/28 suites exit 0, identical=52/expected=37/unexplained=4). Do not infer the broader suite is green from this session. No unit_status or gate changed this session (nothing new was independently verified end-to-end; P5 gate stays closed until the owner runs the seeder AND a session re-verifies the real sweep). Production .env untouched; all flags stayed off/unset throughout; seeder shown, never run.
